narrative.analyst_commentary: Empire Company Limited posted decent operating results in the first half of fiscal 2025. (Year concludes on May 2, 2026.) During the first six months, sales edged up 2.1% from the same span in fiscal 2024, to an aggregate of $16.25 billion. Food revenues increased 3.0% year to date, driven by growth across the business and expansion of the wholesale distribution network. Fuel sales dropped 10.0% during the first two quarters, which management attributed to lower prices due to the removal of a government carbon tax. All told, adjusted earnings were essentially even through the November period, with the comparable prior-year figure, at a combined $1.60 per share. Our topand bottom-line estimates for full-year fiscal 2025 are set at $32.0 billion and $3.15 per share, respectively. The grocer continues to invest in the store base. Capital expenditures were $343 million during the first half of fiscal 2025, versus $301 million in the likeperiod last year. The bulk of the funds was used for store renovations and the construction of new locations, as well as investments in advanced analytics technology. Leadership is targeting $850 million in expenditures for the full year, up from $721 million in fiscal 2024. The balance sheet is in great shape. Total debt increased 6.2% in the recent November term, from the year-earlier level, to $1.27 billion. The debt-to-total capital ratio matched the prior-year reading, at a healthy 16%. The lion’s share of the additional capital was deployed to expand and improve the company’s sizable store base and distribution network. Fiscal 2026 ought to be another productive year for Empire Company. Our respective sales and adjusted earnings forecasts for next year, at $33.0 billion and $3.35 per share, would represent annual gains of 3%–4% and 6%–7%. As the second largest grocer in Canada, some of the progress we anticipate for the company in the coming years will depend on the health of the economy north of the border. The stock lacks investment appeal at present. The shares are ranked to only mirror the year-ahead market, total return potential to late decade is muted, and the yield is below the Value Line average.
narrative.business: Empire Company Limited is primarily engaged in the food retailing business through its wholly-owned subsidiary Sobeys Inc. Also operates IGA, Safeway, Thrifty Foods, Foodland, and Fresh Co locations. It has over 1,600 retail stores, as well as more than 350 retail fuel locations (Fast Fuel, Safeway, and Shell) operating in every Canadian province. Empire also owns a 41.5% in Crombie REIT, which owns and operates shopping centers and free-standing stores. Acquired Canada Safeway for $5.8 billion in November, 2013. Has more than 125,000 employees. President & CEO: Michael Medline. Chairman: James M. Dickson QC. Inc.: Canada. Address: 115 King Street, Stellarton, Nova Scotia, B0K 1S0, Canada. Tel.: 902-755-4440. Internet: www.empireco.ca.
